findings.issue: The POST handler has no input-validation branch for `title`. In `createTodo`, `const { title } = req.body;` is passed directly into `db.run('INSERT INTO todos (title) VALUES (?)', [title], ...)`, so a request with a missing/empty/invalid title does not return a deterministic 400 response and instead falls through to database behavior or can create an invalid row.
findings.recommendation: Validate `req.body.title` before calling `db.run` and return `400` with a clear error body when `title` is missing, empty, or not a string.

findings.issue: The PUT handler has no input-validation branch for the fields it writes. In `updateTodo`, `const { title, completed } = req.body;` is used directly in `db.run('UPDATE todos SET title = ?, completed = ? WHERE id = ?', [title, completed, id], ...)`, so malformed bodies can update an existing row with invalid values and still return 200.
findings.recommendation: Validate the request body before the UPDATE and return `400` when `title`/`completed` are missing or of the wrong type; only execute the UPDATE for a valid payload.

findings.issue: The controller imports `../models/todo`, but no corresponding file is listed in `spec.files_to_create` or included in `code.files_written`. If that module does not already exist, this file will fail at load time.
findings.recommendation: Confirm `src/models/todo.js` already exists in the repository; if not, add it to the spec/build output so the controller is runnable.

findings.issue: `dependencies_added` is empty even though the feature depends on the runtime having the SQLite stack described in the spec/design. This is suspicious unless `express` and `sqlite3` were already present.
findings.recommendation: Verify the project manifest already includes the required runtime dependencies; otherwise add the missing packages explicitly.

parsed_verdict.summary: The non-existent-id behavior for PUT and DELETE is implemented correctly, and GET/POST success paths align with the listed acceptance criteria. However, the POST and PUT handlers are missing required invalid-input handling, so the implementation is not safe to approve as a complete REST controller.
